content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA consisting of NNN positive integers and a positive integer KKK.

Find whether there exists a subset SSS of the elements of AAA such that the **product** of all elements of SSS is divisible by KKK.

Note that a subset is obtained by deleting some or no elements without changing the order of the remaining elements.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK — the number of elements of AAA and the above mentioned integer KKK.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1,A_2,\ldots,A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ representing the array AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, print on a new line the answer: `YES` if there exists a subset SSS and `NO` otherwise.


Each character of the output may be printed in either uppercase or lowercase, i.e, the strings `Yes`, `YES, `yes`, `yEs` will all be treated as identical.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Consider the subset S=[8]S = [8]S=[8]. The product of all the elements is 888 which is divisible by K=2K = 2K=2.
Test case 222: There exists no subset of AAA such that the product of all elements of the subset is divisible by KKK.
Test case 333: Consider the subset S=[2,3]S = [2, 3]S=[2,3]. The product of all the elements is 666 which is divisible by K=6K = 6K=6.
